item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me laugh out loud when I finally caught on. (Took me longer than I'd rather admit.) Love it, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfectly balanced, as all things should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Scip that is literally a game of freakin' hot potato.

*shits out a steaming pile of upvotes*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love that UIU has taken the role of the only other paranormal organisation we're willing to work with. "Fuck you Serpent's Hand, fuck you GOC, fuck you Church of the Broken God, UIU… you're alright."

My two issues with the backstory were: 1. it's a really inefficient/weird assassination weapon, and 2. I don't think AWCY would make weapons for someone. Then I realised those two "issues" cancel each other out and make perfect sense. Chechen approaches anartist, anartist thinks "screw you, I'm no sell out… actually I do have a pretty cool idea I could use some funding for."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is indeed dumb. It also made me laugh aloud when I got it, after which I got to thoroughly enjoy reading through an acquisition history that could've been set to "Yakety Sax." The yam at the end is gratuitous and kinda anvilicious, but on balance this was too much fun to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The core idea here was pretty barebones, but the documentation about all the shenanigans various GOIs got up to with it was fun to read. One grammar problem that kind of irked me: the bit about Doctor Nottingham burning their hands is a run-on sentence. Other than that, great job! : )

item.title: Re: Ready to the anomalous reflection!
item.content:encoded:
I'll give them two of these:

The mailroom sentence has two "in"'s. Took me a minute to see it myself.

On the "(The) Ukraine" thing: As part of the Soviet Union, it was usually "The Ukraine". As an independent nation, it's "Ukraine". (Compare "the Indonesian archipelago" and "Indonesia") Understandably, it's sort of a big deal at the moment.

item.title: Re: Ready to the anomalous reflection!
item.content:encoded:
is believed to have been lost in in the building’s mailroom

I'm I'm lost lost in in the the my mind my mind… …

This critique doesn't seem to be helpful. It took me forever to figure out what you were talking about here. Instead of trying to be cute, you should probably just say what you mean. Or better, in this case, just correct it, as fixing typographical errors like duplicate words are minor edits anyone can do.

The CI is a sect? I don't think so.

There is no canon, and if the author wants to propose sectarian divisions in the CI they are free to do so (and frankly given the conflicting canon on the CI as well as their basic concept; factions seem highly plausible). However I think the author may actually mean "cell" here.

item.title: Ready to the anomalous reflection!
item.content:encoded:
Okay, mister Solo do you want to see some sorts of a little mistakes? I'm not so let's start!

+ See the abstracted digital misery...
- Do you have some consequences before close it?

though several investigations are currently underway in spontaneous combustion cases in Russia, The Ukraine, and Ireland.

Ukraine it's a name of country they don't nedeed any articles there.

is believed to have been lost in in the building’s mailroom

I'm I'm lost lost in in the the my mind my mind… …

were lost during an incursion on Site-63 by a sect of the Chaos Insurgency. In October of 2012, one half of the sect operatives died as a result of SCP-2635

The CI is a sect? I don't think so.

Now, that's all maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey. 100. Neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*sips hot coco*
A magical hot potato that blows people up…. Doctor Bright should be kept away from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was about to downvote this until I recognized the joke/concept. As it is, I really like the concept, but I dislike the execution. The article wasn't interesting to me. It seemed very dry. There was no dialogue, no developed characters or character interaction, and there was very little about the SCP itself. All the GoIs seemed just shoehorned in without any explanation. The incident history, which made up about half the article, could be summarized as "MC&D did what it always does. GRU-P did what it always does. The rest of the GoIs did what they always do." It was basically that, except a lot more long and rambling, and I was bored after the first one or two GoIs.

I would make a few changes to this article. I would add some more stuff about the SCP itself. Who was the person who made it and what were the circumstances behind its' creation. How was the containment agreement brokered between the Foundation and the UIU? How does the SCP define "ownership" and "organization"? The incident history could work, but I would put it in a collapsible at the bottom or just make it its' own separate linked page, and make it more of a story instead of a flat description of events. If you made these changes, I think that this would be an amazing article. Or you could make a good -J out of it if you made it a full-on humor/parody article.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the big question: what happens if somebody eats this thing? It wouldn't really be in their possession after a few hours/days. Nor would it be a single entity anymore; there'd be potato fractions all over the world in various sewer systems, fertilizer plants, tiny bits of it in the ocean…is there a minimum size at which it stops being anomalous? Actually, we don't know that simply cutting it in half won't neutralize it and make all the previous questions moot.

I understand why they're not testing such things, of course, but…out-of-universe curiosity.

I also have to wonder how many potato skips we have by now. We have a tag for cactus-related skips, so if we have more potatoes than cacti, we may be overdue for a potato tag…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a side-note, that's similar to how the French wiki uses Object Classes! : D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah, just something I wanted to put in there for flavor. Essentially the yellow subclassification denotes that an outside entity is integral to containment.

I'm just really into obscure object classes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with the object class having a specification of "Yellow"? Is that some joke that's sailing over my head?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took me five seconds to realize how awesome this SCP is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Come on guys, that’s cheating. You're supposed to pass it around, let everyone have a turn! Not cool."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a fruit at The Weed Temperature

It's a vegetable at The Weed Temperature. (As far as I know)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually liked the idea, but only insofar as "innocuous-seeming thing that kills you horribly" is a kind of article that I enjoy the hell out of. I didn't get the pun and, when I realized what it was, found it to be stupid-stupid, rather than good-stupid. The AWCY connection for no reason other than "this needs a note" was pretty dang tiresome in about 2012. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a duplicate image here, Mr._Red_Potato.jpg. Might want to delete that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, apparently, hiding it in an unused locker belonging to the Foundation worked just as well as actually giving it to someone, so…

Honestly I think planting it in the building and running off is cheating, and that you gotta actually give it to someone, but that's just my opinion on the "game" so take that as you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are certain ideas and concepts that I like to call as "low hanging fruits". Usually, those are ideas so silly and/or obvious that most people avoid them until someone just takes the chance and publish them. I am not against of these ideas as long as you deliver a proper narrative along with it -and that doesn't happen as often as I'd like by the way-. This one happens to be entertaining, and the narrative, while shallow, is effective delivering the message. I also think this skip is absurd as hell, but that plays in favor in my book. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed to read "2016". I was playing around with the time line a lot for this article and that one slipped past me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate myself for not getting this at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't work out how you'd use this as an assassination method. You'd need to give someone a potato and then expect them to keep, but not eat, said potato for an entire year.

Also what happens to people who are part of more than one organisation. Say it's given to a CI member but he's also the exec for the local board game society?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In April of 2008, a single yam was found on the desk of Doctor Nottingham, Site Director of Site-58.

I'm afraid if I had a misconception, but shouldn't it be "In April of 2018"? I suppose that yam was found after 2635's proper containment procedure was established.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fucking hot potato.

God damn it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not planning on it, but who knows?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you going to keep updating this so that other GoIs can join in one the fun?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last log is much improved since I last saw it, conveying a much more frenetic and hectic process befitting the anomaly. The note at the end was a nice touch as well. It's still a very basic premise, but it's got enough detail and narrative to be enjoyable. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is sorta dumb.

I think the only thing holding me off from a downvote is that the Foundation is cooperating with the UIU to contain it. But seriously, GoI hot potato. So dumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for getting your criticism wrong, I usually mix absurd with that.

Also sorry solo.

EDIT : even to this day, I'm still embarrassed by this, lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't trying to go for in-depth critique since 100% of my problems are covered by Kalinin and I would more or less be parroting him. You're right that my critique wasn't "don't exaggerate" things too much. It was that this is too absurd to enjoy on any level. Examples:

1) If all of these GOIs are existing and have the ability to pass around this hot potato to each other, why not actually elaborate or go in-depth on either the (supposed) intristic humor this carries? Because I am to presume this is supposed to be funny, as I can't take "hot potato but as an SCP" seriously.

2) If your intent was for me to take this seriously, I can't when this was created by an extreme leftist who is a part of AWCY? that got commissioned to make this and also hates Russia, and also there are four other pieces. One of which is implied to be a fruit at The Weed Temperature.

3) The mere fact that you have a grabbag of groups diminishes any one of their appearances and the total impact they are to bring. They exist soley to be part of the punchline, and distract from the inherent absurdity of a leftist hot potato as an anomaly.

This is more of an elaboration on what Kalinin has said, and granted, I might have been well to voice at least any of these critiques to begin with. But "too absurd" is a valid critique, as the article hinges on its absurdity and how willing the reader is to shut off their brain to enjoy it. And I can't do that for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. Yes, "try not to exaggerate things so much" is fine crit, but that isn't what was said, and I don't feel like it's particularly applicable to my article.
B. If you did not even read the article you really aren't in a position to talk about critiquing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think "try not to exaggerate things so much" is a good critique.

I'm sure I would like this SCP if I read it but yeah. It's her opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that's… entirely unhelpful, but alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is too absurd even for me to get into.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hey you posted it, noice.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Article based on a frankly dumb title joke, AWCY? once again serving the role of terrorist organization that's associated with art for some reason, a bunch of GoIs that all somehow manage to keep their activities secret while killing each other, and an ending featuring a thing with a note taped to it. There is absolutely no way I can not downvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This wouldn't have worked nearly as well if all of the GoIs weren't brought in to play their parts, so thank you for not skimping there. Also, I'm trying to imagine the stress of that first day ownership is temporarily transferred over to the UIU, not knowing at that point if it's 100% going to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this, mostly because I didn't see the title before I read the article. I read it as a fun example of how an item can reveal so much about the people who hold it; AWCY making something as a political statement, MC&D using it as a renewable profit source, all the militarized GoIs using it as a weapon, and the Foundation understanding it as another item to be contained.

But then I got to the end note and saw the title and the humor brought it up another level. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The solution to the hot potato is incredibly satisfying. +1

item.title: So Dumb...
item.content:encoded:
… And yet I cant keep myself from loving it.

Your brilliant, sir, brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love me some potato skips.

Also grammar thing: "and allowing Foundation recovery many stolen anomalies in the Chaos Insurgency’s possession" should be something like "and allowing the Foundation to recover many stolen anomalies in the Chaos Insurgency's possession"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking brilliant. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Share with the world!
Heh, might be a good solution to overpopulation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I am mildly exasperated by the sheer abundance of GoI references (when I'm usually the type who would welcome them), I am at least satisfied by the Foundation's ultimate solution to the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Said it in chat, I'll say it here, I think this is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This works as a regular and as a joke, I think. :D

HOT POTATO! COMIN' THROUGH!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2635 makes the annual park picnics with the Foundation and other GOI all the better. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A hot potato. I can't with you.

+1

PS: There's a spacing typo. "sectoperatives" should be "sect operatives"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is sorta dumb. Hope ya like it. Thanks to everyone who helped out.
